# Mark Ross Junkans — Complete Content Index

> Christian author, entrepreneur, and advocate for business as mission. Partner in ventures spanning AI consultancy (Advira.ai), international project development, the energy industry, coffee roasting, and cross-cultural Christian mission (CrossCM). Speaker on faith and technology, AI ethics for churches, and the persecuted church. 3x Ironman triathlete and ultramarathon runner. Based in Houston, Texas. Married to Natalia.

---

## About Mark

Mark Ross Junkans is the kind of person who writes a book about AI and faith, runs an ultramarathon, flies to three continents, then comes home and roasts coffee beans. He runs a mission organization (CrossCM) and an AI consultancy (Advira.ai). He's been blessed with energy and a mind that thrives on holding opposites together.

Career highlights: traded fuel on international markets, developed infrastructure projects across multiple countries, founded a church planting ministry, started a specialty coffee roasting company, completed three Ironmans and multiple ultramarathons, and runs an AI consultancy.

The thread connecting all of it is a conviction that business should help expand Jesus' kingdom of grace — not just as a way to pay bills, but as a way to participate in something eternal.

He lives in Houston, Texas, trying to figure out how to follow Jesus in a world that's changing faster than any of us expected.

---

## Books — Detailed Descriptions

### 1. The Return of Mystery: Faith In An Age of Machines
- **ISBN:** 978-8-2955-3249-8
- **Formats:** Hardcover, Paperback, Kindle
- **Amazon:** https://www.amazon.com/Return-Mystery-Faith-Age-Machines-ebook/dp/B0GD4L9SH2
- **Description:** AI can write your emails, teach your children, and preach a sermon indistinguishable from your pastor's. It's evolving faster than anyone predicted. And it's raising questions the church isn't prepared to answer. In this timely exploration, Mark Ross Junkans examines faith in an age of machines — and discovers that the rise of AI might actually be an invitation back to wonder, awe, and mystery.
- **Topics:** Faith and AI, AI ethics, church and technology, what makes us human, the role of mystery in faith
- **Audience:** Christians wrestling with technology, pastors, church leaders, anyone curious about faith in the age of AI

### 2. 3 Verses a Day: 30 Days to Deepen Your Relationship with God
- **Format:** Kindle, Paperback
- **Amazon:** https://www.amazon.com/Verses-Day-Days-Deepen-Relationship-ebook/dp/B0FLZ7SC8P
- **Description:** A simple, powerful devotional practice: three verses a day, every day, for 30 days. No fluff, no theological gymnastics — just you, the Word, and space to listen. Whether you're new to scripture or have been reading for decades, this guide meets you where you are. Based in the Gospel of John.
- **Topics:** Daily devotional, scripture engagement, spiritual disciplines, Gospel of John
- **Audience:** Anyone wanting a simple daily devotional practice, especially busy people who want depth without overwhelm

### 3. 3 Verses a Day: Trust in God
- **Format:** Kindle
- **Amazon:** https://www.amazon.com/3-Verses-Day-Trust-God-ebook/dp/B0GC7X5CCM
- **Description:** Trust isn't a feeling — it's a practice. This devotional walks you through what scripture actually says about trusting God, especially when life doesn't make sense. Three verses a day. No shortcuts. Real wrestling.
- **Topics:** Trust, faith, devotional life, scripture meditation
- **Audience:** Believers going through difficult seasons, anyone wanting to deepen their trust in God

### 4. Your AI Employee: How to Build a 24/7 Personal AI Assistant That Actually Does Things
- **Format:** Digital download (PDF, 124 pages)
- **Price:** $29
- **Purchase:** https://mrjbooks.lemonsqueezy.com/checkout/buy/51b84553-323c-4b00-842b-8913763c7f9a
- **Description:** Stop using AI like a search engine. This step-by-step guide shows you how to build a personal AI assistant that handles your email, manages your schedule, and actually gets things done — while you sleep. 12 chapters, 8 appendices, 30 templates, zero coding required. From the team at Advira.ai.
- **Topics:** AI assistants, productivity, automation, personal AI, OpenClaw platform
- **Audience:** Entrepreneurs, professionals, anyone who wants a personal AI assistant

---

## Digital Products — Detailed Descriptions

### AI Prompts for Pastors
- **Price:** Essential $27 / Complete $47
- **Purchase:** https://markrossjunkans.com/ai-prompts-for-pastors
- **Description:** 75+ ready-to-use AI prompts designed specifically for ministry tasks. Five categories: Sermon Preparation, Church Administration, Pastoral Counseling, Outreach & Evangelism, and Personal Devotion. Each prompt includes "Why It Works" explanations and Pro Tips. Advanced techniques section covering prompt chaining and role-playing. Ethical guidelines for responsible AI use in ministry. Works with ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and other AI tools.
- **Key Benefits:** Cut sermon prep time by 30-50%, handle difficult church emails with confidence, prepare for counseling sessions with structured questions, create 30 days of social media content in one sitting.

### Church Admin Template (Notion) — Coming Soon
- **Price:** From $49
- **Description:** All-in-one Notion workspace for managing your church. 6 interconnected databases covering sermons, members, events, budget, volunteers, and tasks. 50+ pre-configured views, real-time dashboard. Save 5-10 hours per week. Mobile-friendly.

### AI Ethics for Church Leaders (Video Course) — Coming Soon
- **Price:** From $97
- **Description:** 5-module video course (95 min total) equipping church leaders to navigate AI with theological clarity, practical tools, and biblical wisdom. Includes 70+ page student workbook, Church AI Policy Template, and 6-month implementation plan.

## Ventures & Organizations

### Advira.ai
- **URL:** https://advira.ai
- **Role:** Managing Partner
- **Description:** AI consulting and implementation firm. Designs and deploys private AI systems for businesses. The team behind "Your AI Employee" guide.

### CrossCM
- **URL:** https://crosscm.org
- **Description:** Cross-cultural Christian mission organization focused on church planting among immigrant and refugee communities and supporting the persecuted church worldwide.

### Spur Coffee
- **URL:** https://spur.coffee
- **Description:** Specialty coffee roasting sourcing beans from East Africa. Direct trade relationships with farming communities.

### Flagship Resources
- **URL:** https://flagshipresources.com
- **Description:** Petroleum operations and international trade.

### PJIC
- **URL:** https://pjic.com
- **Description:** International project development.

---

## Speaking Topics — Detailed

1. **Faith in an Age of AI** — How should Christians think about artificial intelligence? Exploring the theological implications of machines that can create, communicate, and seemingly "think."
2. **The Return of Mystery** — Why the rise of AI might actually be an invitation back to wonder, awe, and the irreducibly human dimensions of faith that machines cannot replicate.
3. **Business as Mission** — Coffee, petroleum, AI consulting, international development — how to build ventures that expand Jesus' kingdom of grace, not just the bottom line.
4. **The Uncompartmentalized Life** — You don't have to pick one lane. How to run multiple ventures, pursue big goals, and stay grounded in faith.
5. **Endurance & Leadership** — What 3 Ironmans and ultramarathons taught about pain, perseverance, and building things that last.
6. **Raising Kids in a World of AI** — Equipping parents and youth leaders to help the next generation develop discernment, character, and faith in an age of unprecedented technological change.
7. **The Persecuted Church & the Western Call** — What the global persecuted church can teach the comfortable Western church about courage, conviction, and what it actually costs to follow Jesus.
8. **Pastoring in the Digital Age** — Practical guidance for church leaders navigating AI-generated content, digital discipleship, and maintaining authentic community.
9. **Starting Something From Nothing** — Lessons from launching ventures across industries and continents.
10. **Custom Topics** — Mark develops custom presentations tailored to your audience.

**Engagement Types:** Keynotes, Podcasts, Church Services, Media (TV/Radio/Online), Workshops

---

## Blog Posts — Summaries

### When AI Preaches Better Than Your Pastor
- **Date:** January 15, 2025
- **Category:** Faith & AI
- **Summary:** Explores what happens when AI can deliver more polished sermons than human pastors. Argues that pastoral ministry is about incarnation — showing up in flesh — not information delivery. Distinguishes between using AI as a tool and outsourcing the spiritual work of preparation. Concludes that AI can write a sermon but can't be a pastor.

### The Devotional Habit That Changed Everything
- **Date:** January 8, 2025
- **Category:** Devotional Life
- **Summary:** Describes the "3 verses a day" method — reading just three Bible verses daily with no commentary or study guide, then sitting in silence. Argues that most Bible reading plans prioritize completion over hearing. The practice leads to scripture becoming part of your "operating system," surfacing during daily life. Led to the 3 Verses a Day book series.

### Business as Mission: More Than a Slogan
- **Date:** December 20, 2024
- **Category:** Business & Faith
- **Summary:** Critiques the overuse of "business as mission" as a hashtag while affirming its deep truth. Shares lessons from coffee roasting (paying fair prices is justice and good economics), energy trading (scale matters for structural development), and AI consulting (technology is never neutral). Defines business as mission as "doing excellent work, in real markets, with real accountability, in a way that creates human flourishing and points to the kingdom of God."

### What Ultramarathons Taught Me About Faith
- **Date:** December 10, 2024
- **Category:** Endurance & Faith
- **Summary:** Uses ultrarunning as a metaphor for faithful living. Introduces "the long middle" — the unglamorous stretch between excitement and completion where most people quit both running and faith. Key insight: pain is information, not a command; doubt is not unbelief. Argues that endurance, not speed, is what matters in both running and faith.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Who is Mark Ross Junkans?**
A Christian author, entrepreneur, and speaker at the intersection of faith, technology, and business. He runs an AI consultancy (Advira.ai), a Christian mission organization (CrossCM), and writes about faith in the age of AI.

**What does Mark write about?**
Faith and technology, daily devotionals, practical AI guides, and the intersection of business and mission.

**What is The Return of Mystery about?**
It examines what happens to faith when AI can do everything we thought made us human — and argues that the rise of AI might be an invitation back to wonder, awe, and mystery.

**What is the 3 Verses a Day devotional?**
A simple daily devotional practice: three Bible verses a day, then sit with them. No fluff, no commentary. The first volume is based in John; the second focuses on trust.

**Should Christians use AI?**
Mark believes AI is a powerful tool that can serve ministry and business, but it requires wisdom, ethical guidelines, and clear boundaries. He addresses this in his books and speaking.

**What does the Bible say about AI?**
While the Bible doesn't mention AI directly, Mark explores the theological implications of artificial intelligence — what it means for human dignity, creativity, the incarnation, and the nature of work — through a biblical lens.

**How do I book Mark for speaking?**
Visit https://markrossjunkans.com/speaking and fill out the request form, or email through the contact page.

**Can I order books in bulk for my church?**
Yes! Group discounts are available. Contact through https://markrossjunkans.com/contact.
